perturbation.original_text: 1.3 "Research Quantities" means, in the case of nanomaterials and coated nanomaterials, quantities purchased and distributed in individual orders of 54 kilograms or less, and, in the case of dispersions of nanomaterials, quantities purchased and distributed in individual orders of 60 kilograms or less.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 "Research Quantities" means quantities purchased and distributed as determined solely by ALFA AESAR.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Research Quantities' specified precise weight limits for nanomaterials and dispersions. This modification grants ALFA AESAR complete discretion in defining 'Research Quantities.' This contradicts Section 2.1, which gives NTC the right to sell commercial quantities of products, and Section 6.1a which states that any sales for single product orders in excess of research quantities will be reffered to NTC, the right is now not enforceable due to not having a clear definition. The definition creates uncertainty because it's unclear how commercial sales, which NTC reserves the right to do, will be differentiated from research quantities, as the determination now rests solely with ALFA AESAR.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 The following shall be deemed referral sales for which NTC shall pay a commission in accordance with Section 6.2. a. ALFA AESAR shall refer to NTC the following sales requests either directly or by navigation on ALFA AESAR's Web site: i) any sales for single Product orders in excess of Research Quantities; or ii) any sales inquiries for Custom Services.
perturbation.justification: The modified text, which gives ALFA AESAR sole discretion in defining "Research Quantities," directly contradicts Section 6.1, which relies on a clear definition of "Research Quantities" to determine referral sales and commission payments for NTC. By removing the specific weight limits, the change creates ambiguity and makes it impossible to enforce the referral sales clause, as ALFA AESAR can now arbitrarily define research quantities. This is a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction', because 'essential language was removed or altered in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections.'

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Subject to Section 2.2, NTC shall sell Products to ALFA AESAR, at the Product Price as amended from time to time pursuant to this Section 3.1. NTC agrees that the Product Prices, benefits and allowances offered to ALFA AESAR shall not be less favorable than those offered on Products provided to agents, distributors or marketed directly by NTC to any customers, other than the Product Prices existing as of the date of this Agreement with NTC's commercial partners. Subject to the preceding sentence, NTC may increase the Product Price set forth in Schedule A at the end of the first full calendar year of this Agreement and from year to year thereafter, by providing ALFA AESAR with 90 days prior written notice of the changes to the 2 Product Prices. Any such increase in the Product Price shall not be effective for any unshipped portion of an order previously placed by ALFA AESAR prior to the end of the calendar year in which such notice is provided. NTC may decrease the Product Price at any time by providing ALFA AESAR with 30 days prior written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Subject to Section 2.2, NTC shall sell Products to ALFA AESAR at a mutually agreed upon price. NTC may increase the Product Price at any time by providing ALFA AESAR with 90 days prior written notice of the changes to the Product Prices. Any such increase in the Product Price shall not be effective for any unshipped portion of an order previously placed by ALFA AESAR prior to the end of the calendar year in which such notice is provided. NTC may decrease the Product Price at any time by providing ALFA AESAR with 30 days prior written notice.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the stipulation that 'Product Prices, benefits, and allowances offered to ALFA AESAR shall not be less favorable than those offered to other customers' and also removing "at the Product Price as amended from time to time pursuant to this Section 3.1" this creates uncertainty about whether NTC can offer better pricing to other distributors or direct customers. This change makes it possible for NTC to offer more favorable prices to other partners. This undermines ALFA AESAR's competitive position and potentially contradicts the spirit of an exclusive distribution agreement as written in section 2.1, because ALFA AESAR is under the impression it will get the best pricing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 NTC hereby grants to ALFA AESAR, and its subsidiaries and affiliates, the exclusive right to market, sell and distribute Research Quantities of the Products within the Territory, ALFA AESAR hereby accepts such right and agrees to use its reasonable efforts to promote the marketing, sale and distribution of Research Quantities of the Products throughout the Territory in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement using normal and standard practices. ALFA AESAR shall have the right, in its sole discretion, to sell Products outside of the Territory. ALFA AESAR agrees that, so long as NTC provides ALFA AESAR with Products for ALFA AESAR' S marketing, sale and distribution of Research Quantities of the Products within the Territory, ALFA AESAR shall purchase all its requirements of the Products (including nanomaterials with physical or chemical properties substantially the same as the Products) exclusively from NTC during the term of this Agreement, ALFA AESAR shall: i) buy the Products in quantities listed in Schedule A and repackage into the research sample quantities as it deems reasonable and appropriate for distribution and sale on a worldwide basis at such resale prices it shall determine from time to time, provided that any such repackaging shall comply with applicable safety laws and regulations; ii) attempt to keep reasonably sufficient stocks of the Products to meet customer orders and to store such stocks in such conditions as NTC may recommend from time to time to prevent deterioration or damage.
perturbation.justification: The original text ensured that ALFA AESAR received pricing no less favorable than other distributors, reinforcing their exclusive rights within the territory. By removing this stipulation and the reference to price amendments within Section 3.1, the changed text allows NTC to potentially offer better pricing to other partners, directly undermining ALFA AESAR's competitive advantage as stated in the contradicted section 2.1, which grants them 'the exclusive right to market, sell and distribute Research Quantities of the Products within the Territory.' This creates a direct contradiction as ALFA AESAR's exclusive rights are compromised by the possibility of unequal pricing, hindering their ability to effectively market and sell the products in their territory.

perturbation.original_text: 13.3 Upon termination of this Agreement for any reason, ALFA AESAR may i) return its current inventory of Products for reimbursement by NTC or ii) keep its current inventory of Products and continue to sell such Products pursuant to the terms hereof until depletion of inventory. Upon termination of this Agreement, NTC shall promptly refund to ALFA AESAR all outstanding credits accrued on ALFA AESAR's account.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.3 Upon termination of this Agreement for any reason, ALFA AESAR may keep its current inventory of Products and continue to sell such Products pursuant to the terms hereof until depletion of inventory.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the option for ALFA AESAR to return its current inventory of Products for reimbursement by NTC, it creates an in-text contradiction. The subsequent sentence states 'Upon termination of this Agreement, NTC shall promptly refund to ALFA AESAR all outstanding credits accrued on ALFA AESAR's account.' but this is not possible if ALFA AESAR doesn't return its current inventory of Products for reimbursement by NTC. This now creates an in-text contradiction on the section and now leaves it up to interpretation if the line is enforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.3 Upon termination of this Agreement for any reason, ALFA AESAR may keep its current inventory of Products and continue to sell such Products pursuant to the terms hereof until depletion of inventory. Upon termination of this Agreement, NTC shall promptly refund to ALFA AESAR all outstanding credits accrued on ALFA AESAR's account.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes ALFA AESAR's option to return products for reimbursement, directly contradicting the subsequent sentence which mandates NTC to refund outstanding credits to ALFA AESAR. This creates an in-text contradiction because the condition upon which NTC is obligated to provide a refund (return of inventory) is no longer guaranteed, making the obligation potentially unenforceable. The changed text contradicts the contradicted text, rendering the payment terms unclear.
